title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Farmingdale, NY | $900-$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Farmingdale, Long Island. Safe, humane bat extraction from attics and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Farmingdale homeowners and business owners facing bat infestations need swift, humane solutions to protect their properties and health. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Farmingdale, from historic Main Street buildings to residential neighborhoods near Bethpage State Park. Our certified technicians safely remove bats and seal entry points to prevent future intrusions, with services ranging from $900 to $2,500 depending on colony size and building structure.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over 15 years of bat removal expertise to Farmingdale, using humane exclusion methods that comply with all New York wildlife protection laws. Our technicians are specially trained in Long Island building structures and provide guaranteed results with comprehensive warranties on all exclusion work.
localContext: Farmingdale's mix of century-old homes near the village center and newer residential developments creates numerous opportunities for bat colonies to establish themselves in attics, soffits, and wall voids. Properties near wooded areas like Bethpage State Park and along Route 109 are particularly susceptible to bat activity, especially during spring and fall migration periods.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Farmingdale?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Farmingdale typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the bat colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in Farmingdale regulated?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat removal in New York is regulated to protect bat populations, particularly during maternity season (May through August) when flightless pups are present. Our licensed technicians follow all state and local regulations, using humane exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not return, never trapping or harming the animals.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Farmingdale home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds in attics or walls at dusk and dawn, accumulation of bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, dark rub marks around small openings under eaves, and sightings of bats entering or exiting your home at sunset. You may also notice a strong ammonia-like odor from guano accumulation.

faqs.question: How do you prevent bats from returning after removal?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive exclusion process includes identifying and sealing all potential entry points (bats can enter through gaps as small as 3/8 inch), installing one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not return, sanitizing affected areas to remove guano and odors, and providing a warranty on our exclusion work to ensure long-term protection for your Farmingdale property.
